title: Creating frequency tables
description: Practice creating frequency tables from small data sets.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent how many goals Hank scored each season.

$\qquad45,43,45,44,44,44,47$  

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of goals | Number of seasons 
:-: | :-: 
$43$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$44$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$45$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
$46$ |[[☃ numeric-input 4]]
$47$ |[[☃ numeric-input 45]]
hints:
There were $\blue3$ seasons where Hank scored $\pink{44}$ goals.

$\qquad45,43,45,\pink{\large{44}},\pink{\large{44}},\pink{\large{44}},47$  

Number of goals|Number of seasons
:-:  | :-:
$43$|$?$
$\pink{44}$|$\blue3$
$45$|$?$
$46$|$?$
$47$|$?$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of parking spots at each of the houses on Lombardi Avenue.

$\qquad2,1,2,0,2,4, 3$  

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of parking spots | Number of houses
:-: | :-: 
$0$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$1$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$2$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $3$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
$4$  |[[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints:
There is $\blue1$ house with $\pink{0}$ parking spots.

$\qquad2,1,2,\pink{\large{0}},2,4$  

Number of parking spots | Number of houses
:-: | :-:
$\pink0$ | $\blue1$
$1$ | $?$ 
$2$ |  $?$
$3$ | $?$
$4$ | $?$

Number of parking spots | Number of houses
:-: | :-:
$0$ | $1$
$1$ | $1$ 
$2$ |  $3$
$3$ | $1$
$4$ | $1$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of holes that moths ate in each of Grandma Marion's dresses.

$\qquad7,8,8,5,7,8$  

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of holes | Number of dresses
:-: | :-: 
$5$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$6$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$7$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $8$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
$\blue{2}$ of Grandma Marion's dresses had $\pink7$ holes eaten in them.  

$\qquad\pink{\large{7}},8,8,5,\pink{\large{7}},8$  

Number of holes | Number of dresses
:-: | :-:
$5$ | $?$
$6$ | $?$ 
$\pink7$ | $\blue2$
$8$|$?$

Number of holes | Number of dresses
:-: | :-:
$5$ | $1$
$6$ | $0$ 
$7$ | $2$
$8$|$3$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of pictures that Kai has had published in each of the local newspapers.

$\qquad4,3,0,3,1,0$   

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of pictures | Number of newspapers
:-: | :-: 
$0$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$1$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$2$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $3$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
 $4$ | [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints:
There is $\blue{1}$ newspaper in which Kai has had $\pink4$ pictures published.

$\qquad\pink{\large{4}},3,0,3,1,0$ 

Number of pictures | Number of newspapers
:-: | :-:
$0$ | $?$
$1$ | $?$ 
$2$ | $?$
$3$|$?$
$\pink4$|$\blue1$

Number of pictures | Number of newspapers
:-: | :-:
$0$ | $2$
$1$ | $1$ 
$2$ | $0$
$3$|$2$
$4$|$1$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of players on the Russian Bears volleyball team that were injured in each match this year.

$\qquad1,2,2,1, 2,1$  

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of injured players | Number of matches
:-: | :-: 
$0$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$1$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$2$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
There were $\blue{3}$ matches this year where $\pink2$ players were injured.

$\qquad1,\pink{\large{2}},\pink{\large{2}},1, \pink{\large{2}}, 1$  
 
Number of injured players | Number of matches
:-: | :-:
$0$ | $?$
$1$ | $?$ 
$\pink2$ | $\blue3$

Number of injured players | Number of matches
:-: | :-:
$0$ | $0$
$1$ | $3$ 
$2$ | $3$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent how many fish Hugo caught in each tournament.

$\qquad27,27,28,28,25,26,27$   

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of fish | Number of tournaments
:-: | :-: 
$25$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$26$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$27$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $28$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
There were $\blue{3}$ tournaments where Hugo caught $\pink{27}$ fish.

$\qquad\pink{\large{27}},\pink{\large{27}},28,28,25,26,\pink{\large{27}}$  

Number of fish | Number of tournaments
:-: | :-: 
$25$ | $?$
$26$ | $?$
$\pink{27}$ |$\blue{3}$
 $28$ | $?$

Number of fish | Number of tournaments
:-: | :-: 
$25$ | $1$
$26$ | $1$
$27$ |$3$
 $28$ | $2$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of trick-or-treaters James had each year since he started putting pumpkins in his yard. 

$\qquad54,56,53,54,56,55$  

**Using this data, complete the frequency table.**

Number of trick-or-treaters|Number of years
:-: | :-: 
$52$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$53$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$54$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $55$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
 $56$ | [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints:
There were $\blue{2}$ years where James had $\pink{56}$ trick-or-treaters. 

$\qquad54,\pink{\large{56}},53,54,\pink{\large{56}},55$  

Number of trick-or-treaters|Number of years
:-:  | :-:
$52$|$?$
$53$|$?$
$54$|$?$
$55$|$?$
$\pink{56}$|$\blue{2}$

Number of trick-or-treaters|Number of years
:-:  | :-:
$52$|$0$
$53$|$1$
$54$|$2$
$55$|$1$
$56$|$2$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent the number of jars of honey Martha the Bear consumed each day this week.

$\qquad4,4,5,2,2,3, 4$  

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of jars of honey| Number of days
:-:  | :-:
$2$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$3$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$4$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $5$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
There were $\blue3$ days where Martha the Bear consumed $\pink{4}$ jars of honey.

$\qquad\pink{\large{4}},\pink{\large{4}},5,2,2,3, \pink{\large{4}}$  

Number of jars of honey| Number of days
:-:  | :-:
$2$|$?$
$3$|$?$
$\pink4$|$\blue3$
$5$|$?$

Number of jars of honey| Number of days
:-:  | :-:
$2$|$2$
$3$|$1$
$4$|$3$
$5$|$1$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following data points represent how many push-ups Curt did each night last week.

$\qquad 25, 23,  24, 25, 25, 25, 25$


**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of push-ups |  Number of nights
:-:  | :-:
 $23$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
 $24$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
 $25$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
There were $\blue5$ nights where Curt did $\pink{25}$ push-ups.

$\qquad  \pink{\large{25}}, 23,  24,  \pink{\large{25}},  \pink{\large{25}},  \pink{\large{25}}, \pink{\large{25}}$

Number of push-ups |  Number of nights
:-:  | :-:
$23$ | $?$
$24$ | $?$
$\pink{25}$ | $\blue5$
Number of push-ups |  Number of nights
:-:  | :-:
$23$ | $1$
$24$ | $1$
$25$ | $5$

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:
The data below shows how old each of Brad's eight kids were when they started kindergarten.  


[[☃ image 1]]

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Age (in years) | Number of kids
:-: | :-: 
$3$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$4$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$5$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
There was $\blue1$ kid who was $\pink{3}$ years old when she started kindergarten.  


[[☃ image 1]]

Age (in years) | Number of kids
:-: | :-: 
$\pink3$ | $\blue1$
$4$ | $?$ 
$5$ | $?$

Age (in years) | Number of kids
:-: | :-:
$3$ | $1$
$4$ | $5$ 
$5$ | $2$

options.alt: A table with 2 rows and 4 columns. On the top row from left to right are the numbers: 4, 4, 5, and 4. On the bottom row from left to right are the numbers 5, 4, 3, and 4.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tank is a car salesman at EH Auto Dealer.  The data below shows how many cars he sold  in each of the last nine months.  


[[☃ image 1]]

**Using this data, create a frequency table.**

Number of cars sold|Number of months
:-:  | :-:
$28$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$29$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$30$ |[[☃ numeric-input 3]]
 $31$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
There were $\blue2$ months where Tank sold $\pink{31}$ cars.  


[[☃ image 1]]

Number of cars sold|Number of months
:-:  | :-:
$28$|$?$
$29$|$?$
$30$|$?$
$\pink{31}$|$\blue2$

Number of cars sold|Number of months
:-:  | :-:
$28$|$3$
$29$|$3$
$30$|$1$
$31$|$2$
